meta.research_confidence: Very High - Information corroborated across multiple official and educational sources including NEB curriculum documents, leading educational platforms, and past paper analyses.
meta.notes: The syllabus is based on the NEB curriculum for subject code 201. It is also used for the HISSAN central examinations. The total working hours are 160 (128 theory + 32 practical). The final theoretical exam is for 75 marks, while the practical exam accounts for 25 marks.

subtopics: Importance and scope of chemistry
Atoms, molecules, relative masses of atoms and molecules
Atomic mass unit (amu)
Radicals, molecular formula, empirical formula
Percentage composition from a molecular formula

important_numericals: Percentage composition calculation from molecular formula

subtopics: Laws of stoichiometry
Law of conservation of mass
Law of constant composition
Law of multiple proportions
Law of reciprocal proportions
Gay-Lussac's law of gaseous volumes
Molecular mass and vapour density
Molecular mass and volume of gas
Molecular mass and number of particles
Mole and its relation with mass, volume, and number of particles
Calculations based on mole concept
Limiting reactant and excess reactant
Theoretical yield, experimental yield, and % yield
Calculation of empirical and molecular formula from % composition

important_numericals: Mole concept calculations (mass, volume, number of particles)

subtopics: Alpha ray scattering experiment

Concept of probability
Quantum numbers
Orbitals and shapes of s and p orbitals

Pauli's exclusion principle
Hund's rule of maximum multiplicity
Electronic configurations of atoms and ions (up to atomic number 30)

important_derivations: Explanation of hydrogen spectrum using Bohr's model

Atomic radii, ionic radii
Ionization energy, electron affinity, electronegativity

subtopics: Ionic bond and its properties

subtopics: Shapes of BeF2, BF3, CH4, CH3Cl, PCl5, SF6, H2O, NH3, CO2, H2S, PH3
Elementary idea of Valence Bond Theory
Hybridization involving s and p orbitals only
Bond characteristics: bond length, ionic character, dipole moment

topics.topic_name: General and electronic concept of oxidation and reduction

Balancing redox reactions by oxidation number and ion-electron (half-reaction) method

Quantitative aspect (Faraday's laws of electrolysis)

important_reactions: Balancing of redox reactions

subtopics: Kinetic theory of gas and its postulates
Gas laws: Boyle's law, Charles' law, Avogadro's law, combined gas equation
Dalton's law of partial pressure, Graham's law of diffusion
Ideal gas and ideal gas equation (PV = nRT)
Universal gas constant and its significance
Deviation of real gas from ideality (solving related numerical problems)
Physical properties of liquids: evaporation and condensation, vapour pressure and boiling point, surface tension and viscosity (qualitative idea only)
Liquid crystals and their applications
Types of solids: amorphous and crystalline solids
Efflorescent, deliquescent, and hygroscopic solids
Crystallization and crystal growth, water of crystallization
Introduction to unit crystal lattice and unit cell

important_numericals: Numericals based on Boyle's, Charles', and combined gas laws
Numericals based on Graham's law of diffusion
Numericals based on Dalton's law of partial pressure
important_derivations: Derivation of Graham's law of diffusion

subtopics: Dynamic nature of chemical equilibrium

subtopics: Expression for equilibrium constant and its importance
Relationship between Kp and Kc

topics.topic_name: Le Chatelier's Principle and its applications
subtopics: Effect of concentration, temperature, and pressure on equilibrium state

important_derivations: Derivation of relationship between Kp and Kc
Derivation of law of mass action

subtopics: Chemistry of atomic and nascent hydrogen
Isotopes of hydrogen and their uses
Application of hydrogen as fuel
Heavy water and its applications
Definition of allotropy and examples
Types of oxides (acidic, basic, neutral, amphoteric, peroxide, and mixed oxides)
Applications of hydrogen peroxide; Medical and industrial application of oxygen
Occurrence, preparation of ozone from oxygen, structure of ozone, test for ozone
Ozone layer depletion (causes, effects, and control measures), uses of ozone
Reason for inertness of nitrogen and active nitrogen
Chemical properties of ammonia [Action with CuSO4 solution, water, FeCl3 solution, Conc. HCl, Mercurous nitrate paper, O2]
Applications of ammonia
Harmful effects of ammonia
Oxy-acids of nitrogen (name and formula)
Chemical properties of nitric acid [HNO3 as an acid and oxidizing agent (action with zinc, magnesium, iron, copper, sulphur, carbon, SO2, and H2S)]
Ring test for nitrate ion
General characteristics of halogens

Comparative study on preparation (no diagram and description required), chemical properties [with water, alkali, ammonia, oxidizing character, bleaching action] and uses of halogens (Cl2, Br2 and I2);
Test for Cl2, Br2 and I2
Comparative study on preparation (no diagram and description required), properties (reducing strength, acidic nature and solubility) and uses of haloacids (HCl, HBr and HI)
Allotropes of carbon (crystalline and amorphous) including fullerenes (structure, general properties and uses only)
Properties (reducing action, reaction with metals and nonmetals) and uses of carbon monoxide
Allotropes of phosphorus (name only)
Preparation (no diagram and description required), properties (basic nature, reducing nature, action with halogens and oxygen) and uses of phosphine
Allotropes of sulphur (name only) and uses of sulphur
Hydrogen sulphide (preparation from Kipp's apparatus with diagram), properties (Acidic nature, reducing nature, analytical reagent) and uses
Sulphur dioxide its properties (acidic nature, reducing nature, oxidising nature and bleaching action) and uses
Sulphuric acid and its properties (acidic nature, oxidising nature, dehydrating nature) and uses
Sodium thiosulphate (formula and uses)

important_reactions: Reactions of ammonia with various reagents (CuSO4, Cl2, etc.)
Reactions of nitric acid as an oxidizing agent
Preparation of hydrogen sulphide in Kipp's apparatus
Ring test for nitrate ion
Tests for halogens (Cl2, Br2, I2)

subtopics: Definition of metallurgy and its types (hydrometallurgy, pyrometallurgy, electrometallurgy)

Refining of metals (poling and electro-refinement)
General characteristics of alkali metals
Sodium [extraction from Down's process, properties (action with Oxygen, water, acids nonmetals and ammonia) and uses]
Properties (precipitation reaction and action with carbon mon.oxide) and uses of sodium hydroxide
Properties (action with CO2, SO2, water, precipitation reactions) and uses of sodium carbonate
General characteristics of alkaline earth metals
Molecular formula and uses of (quick lime, bleaching powder, magnesia, plaster of paris and epsom salt)
Solubility of hydroxides, carbonates and sulphates of alkaline earth metals (general trend with explanation)
Stability of carbonate and nitrate of alkaline earth metals (general trend with explanation)

important_reactions: Extraction of sodium by Down's process
Action of sodium with water and ammonia
Action of sodium hydroxide with CO2, SO2
Conversion of sodium carbonate to sodium hydroxide

subtopics: Introduction of Micro and macro nutrients

topics.topic_name: Introduction to organic chemistry and organic compounds
subtopics: Reasons for the separate study of organic compounds from inorganic compounds

subtopics: Classification of organic compounds
Alkyl groups, functional groups and homologous series
Idea of structural formula, contracted formula and bond line structural formula

subtopics: IUPAC Nomenclature of Organic Compounds (upto chain having 6 carbon atoms)
Detection of N, S and halogens by Lassaigne's test

Concept of geometrical isomerism (cis & trans) & optical isomerism (d & l form)
Homolytic and heterolytic fission
Electrophiles, nucleophiles and free- radicals
Inductive effect: +I and –I effect
Resonance effect: +R and –R effect

important_reactions: Lassaigne's test for detection of elements

subtopics: Preparation from haloalkanes (Reduction and Wurtz reaction), Decarboxylation, Catalytic hydrogenation of alkene and alkyne
Chemical properties: Substitution reactions (halogenation, nitration & sulphonation only), oxidation of ethane
Alkenes: Preparation by Dehydration of alcohol, Dehydrohalogenation, Catalytic hydrogenation of alkyne
Chemical properties: Addition reaction with HX (Markovnikov's addition and peroxide effect), H2O, O3, H2SO4 only
Alkynes: Preparation from carbon and hydrogen, 1,2 dibromoethane, chloroform/iodoform only
Chemical properties: Addition reaction with (H2, HX, H2O), Acidic nature (action with Sodium, ammoniacal AgNO3 and ammoniacal Cu2Cl2)
Test of unsaturation (ethene & ethyne): bromine water test and Baeyer's test
Comparative studies of physical properties of alkane, alkene and alkyne
Kolbe's electrolysis methods for the preparation of alkane, alkene and alkynes

important_reactions: Wurtz reaction
Kolbe's electrolysis
Bromine water test and Baeyer's test for unsaturation
Addition reactions of alkenes and alkynes

topics.topic_name: Introduction and characteristics of aromatic compounds
subtopics: Huckel's rule of aromaticity
Kekule structure of benzene
Resonance and isomerism
Preparation of benzene from decarboxylation of sodium benzoate, phenol, and ethyne only
Physical properties of benzene
Addition reaction: hydrogen, halogen

Electrophilic substitution reactions: orientation of benzene derivatives (o, m & p), nitration, sulphonation, halogenations, Friedel-Craft's reaction (alkylation and acylation), combustion of benzene (free combustion only) and uses

important_derivations: Understanding the concept of aromaticity and Huckel's rule
important_reactions: Nitration, sulphonation, halogenation, and Friedel-Craft's reaction of benzene
Combustion of benzene

subtopics: Stages in producing a new product
Economics of production, Cash flow in the production cycle
Running a chemical plant, Designing a chemical plant
Continuous and batch processing
Environmental impact of the chemical industry

topics.topic_name: Modern Chemical Manufactures (principle and flow sheet diagram only)
subtopics: Manufacture of ammonia by Haber's process

Manufacture of sulphuric acid by contact process
Manufacture of sodium hydroxide by Diaphragm Cell

important_reactions: Haber's process for ammonia
Ostwald's process for nitric acid
Contact process for sulphuric acid

high_weight_topics.reason: Forms the foundation for many numerical problems and is frequently tested in exams.

high_weight_topics.topic: Atomic Structure (Bohr's Model and Quantum Numbers)
high_weight_topics.reason: Core conceptual topic with both theoretical and numerical questions.
Crucial for understanding molecular shapes, which is a key exam focus.

high_weight_topics.topic: States of Matter (Gas Laws and Kinetic Theory)
high_weight_topics.reason: High weightage numericals and derivations are common from this chapter.
A large volume of content with many reagent-based reactions frequently asked.
Organic chemistry reactions, tests, and mechanisms form a significant part of the exam.
Process diagrams and principles are commonly asked in applied chemistry.

most_important_chapters.why_important: Essential for solving numerical problems across multiple topics and carries high marks.

most_important_chapters.chapter_name: Chemical Bonding and Shapes of Molecules
most_important_chapters.why_important: Combines conceptual understanding with the ability to predict molecular structures, a key skill for the exam.
Major source of derivations and numericals, consistently appearing in exams.
Covers a vast array of reactions, preparations, and properties that are straightforward to score from.
Foundation for organic chemistry reactions and tests, with high weightage in exams.

practical_topics.experiment_name: To study the names and the use of various apparatus used in chemistry laboratory
key_concepts: Familiarity with beakers, flasks, pipettes, burettes, clamps, etc.

practical_topics.experiment_name: To separate insoluble solid from the mixture of soluble and insoluble solid by the process of filtration
To separate volatile and non-volatile components from the mixture by sublimation
key_concepts: Sublimation process, examples like ammonium chloride

practical_topics.experiment_name: To obtain the crystal of sodium chloride by the process of neutralization
key_concepts: Neutralization reaction between acid and base, crystallization

practical_topics.experiment_name: To obtain the precipitate of barium sulphate by the reaction between barium chloride and sulphuric acid
To separate saturated solution of bazaar copper sulphate and to make crystals from it
To separate the components of mixture from calcium carbonate and sand
key_concepts: Differences in chemical properties (e.g., acid reaction with carbonate)

practical_topics.experiment_name: To test ferrous ion in the given aqueous solution and oxidise it to ferric ion
key_concepts: Redox reaction, qualitative analysis of iron

practical_topics.experiment_name: To determine the equivalent weight of the given metal by hydrogen displacement method
To prepare hydrogen gas and study its properties
key_concepts: Preparation of hydrogen (e.g., reaction of zinc with dilute acid), study of its properties

practical_topics.experiment_name: To identify acid radicals present in the given sample
key_concepts: Qualitative analysis of anions (e.g., Cl⁻, SO₄²⁻, CO₃²⁻)

chapters_to_finish_first: Chemical Bonding and Shapes of Molecules
last_minute_revision_topics: Important reactions of organic chemistry (Hydrocarbons, Aromatic Hydrocarbons)
Tests for unsaturation and various ions
Periodic trends in the periodic table
Flow sheet diagrams for chemical manufactures
high_scoring_topics: Laws of stoichiometry and mole concept numericals
Gas laws (Boyle's, Charles', ideal gas law)
Nomenclature and isomerism in organic compounds
Reactions of non-metals (especially ammonia, nitric acid, halogens)
Manufacture of ammonia, nitric acid, and sulphuric acid
